sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking the blue blob.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
changes = [(r, c, before[r][c], after[r][c]) for r in range(64) for c in range(64) if r < len(before) and c < len(before[r]) and before[r][c] != after[r][c]]
print(f"Changed: {len(changes)}")
for r, c, o, n in changes: print(f"  ({r},{c}): {o}->{n}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Only the timer changed. Clicking the blue blob doesn't do anything. Let me reconsider the game.
Let me look at the left panel more carefully. The left panel has a dark square that moves when I click on the panel. The right panel has a checkerboard with yellow shapes. The yellow shapes might represent the target pattern for the control bars.
Let me look at the left panel's dark square position and how it relates to the control bars.
Actually, let me re-examine the game from scratch. In level 1, I clicked on control bars to toggle them from light gray to black. The goal was to make all bars black.
In level 2, there are two panels and two sets of control bars. The left control bars have row 33 black and the rest light gray. The right control bars are all light gray.
Maybe the goal is to make the right control bars match the left control bars pattern. The left pattern is: row 33 black, rest light gray.
But I already tried clicking on the left control bars and they don't respond. So the left control bars are fixed (they're the "template").
Let me try toggling the right row 33 bars to black and see if the level completes.
The right control bars have 4 columns at cols 39, 44, 49, 54. Row 33 has 3-wide bars.
Let me click on each column's row 33 bar to toggle them to black.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try toggling right row 33 bars to black (4 columns)
# Row 33 has 3-wide bars at cols 38-40, 43-45, 48-50, 53-55
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 39},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 44},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 49},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 54}
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
